intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through using the JUnit Migration cleanup to automatically migrate JUnit 3 and JUnit 4 test classes to JUnit 5 (Jupiter) style.

item.title: Understand JUnit 3/4 to JUnit 5 Differences
item.description:
JUnit 5 replaces JUnit 3 and 4 conventions with a modern annotation-based approach:
• extends TestCase → annotations only (no base class needed)
• setUp() → @BeforeEach
• tearDown() → @AfterEach
• @Before → @BeforeEach
• @After → @AfterEach
• @BeforeClass → @BeforeAll
• @AfterClass → @AfterAll
• @Test(expected=...) → assertThrows()
• Assert.assertEquals(msg, expected, actual) → Assertions.assertEquals(expected, actual, msg) (note: message argument moved to last position)
The cleanup handles all of these transformations automatically.

item.title: Add JUnit 5 Dependencies
item.description:
Before running the migration, ensure your project has JUnit 5 (Jupiter) on the classpath:
• For Maven projects: add org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter to your pom.xml dependencies
• For Gradle projects: add testImplementation 'org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter'
• For Eclipse PDE projects: add org.junit.jupiter.api to your Require-Bundle or classpath
The JUnit 5 API must be resolvable before the cleanup can add the correct imports.

item.title: Enable the Cleanup
item.description:
Open the Clean Up dialog to enable the JUnit Migration cleanup:
1. In the Package Explorer, select a Java project containing test classes
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Select Use custom profile and click Configure…
4. On the JUnit Migration (Sandbox) tab, enable the desired migration steps
5. Click OK
Click Perform to open the Source menu.

item.title: Run the Cleanup on a Test Class
item.description:
Apply the migration cleanup to your test classes:
1. Open or select the test Java files to migrate
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Confirm the profile has JUnit Migration (Sandbox) enabled
4. Click Finish
The cleanup transforms annotations, replaces lifecycle methods, and updates assertion calls.

item.title: Review the Changes
item.description:
After migration, inspect the updated test class:
• JUnit 4 imports are replaced with org.junit.jupiter.api.* imports
• @Before / @After annotations are replaced with @BeforeEach / @AfterEach
• @Test(expected=SomeException.class) becomes an assertThrows() call wrapping the test body
• Assert method calls have the message parameter moved to the last position
Run the tests after migration to verify correctness. Use Alt+Shift+X, T to run as JUnit test.
